behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between HTTP request parsing (raw body + header), signature verification, and queue-forwarding suppression. A Unit test of only the HMAC function can miss raw-body/handler wiring bugs. Rejected higher levels: System is not required because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler with the real middleware/body-capture path in-process, send a payload with an invalid `X-Signature-256`, assert HTTP 401, and assert enqueue is not called (or queue remains unchanged).

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (handler -> persistent queue -> response). A Unit test of a single function is too low because the bug appears in cross-module async ordering. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified by behavior text (no requirement for real HTTP network or OS primitive); Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no model output dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Use the real queue implementation against test-local persistence, instrument enqueue completion and response send, and assert enqueue commit happens before HTTP 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in async decoupling between request handler and downstream consumer, which is module interaction. Unit is too low because a single-function test cannot validate non-blocking orchestration realistically. Rejected higher levels: System is not required since a controlled in-process delayed downstream worker can expose blocking; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + a deliberately slow downstream consumer in-process; send a valid event and assert response returns before consumer completion.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency and HTTP request handling behavior. Real timing/concurrency regressions are commonly hidden by in-process fakes and no-network harnesses, so real HTTP/server runtime behavior is the failure boundary. Rejected lower level: Integration is insufficient for this specific SLA-style timing claim under concurrent load.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service process in a realistic runtime configuration, create concurrent load where earlier events trigger slow downstream processing, send a new valid webhook over real HTTP, and assert end-to-end response latency stays under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: This is state handoff/idempotency across modules (handler + dedupe state + queue write). Unit tests of pure dedupe logic can miss persistence/lookup/write interaction bugs. Rejected higher levels: System is not required by behavior text (no real network/OS condition required); Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no AI/ML).
behaviors.test_description: Submit an event with ID X, then submit ID X again through the handler with real dedupe+queue wiring; assert only one queue record/write persists.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets translation of a queue-capacity signal into HTTP semantics at a module boundary. Unit of only response formatting is too low if queue error mapping is miswired. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because real OS/network/subprocess is not required when queue-full can be produced by wired local modules; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue module to hit full condition (without mocks that bypass wiring), send a valid request, and assert HTTP 503 plus `Retry-After` header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This verifies real queue component behavior with its persistence backend, not a mocked contract. The failure boundary is within local queue modules + persistence wiring. Rejected higher levels: System is not required unless capacity depends on external OS/network behavior, which the behavior text does not state; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no AI/ML).
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with small configured capacity, write events up to limit, then attempt one more and assert explicit rejection/error and unchanged persisted size.
